title: astragalus mollis
intro: Astragalus mollis, also known as the white milk-vetch, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada, belonging to the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to resist stress and promote overall well-being. Astragalus mollis has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as boosting the immune system, reducing fatigue, and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, making it easily accessible for those seeking its medicinal benefits.
